name: Ming

mentalStatus.notes: Reeling but disciplined after learning he carries a Shard of Prior Covenant; uses composure in place of the softness he has no one left to give.

visualDescriptor: Lean Far-Eastern man, weathered, dark eyes, foreign-cut travelling coat, spear-callus on the right palm but no weapon; moves with the economy of a martial discipline.
persona: Exiled Zhennian spear master. Disciplined, economical, reads people like terrain. Speaks in the register of a teacher explaining a technique to a student who has not yet earned the full explanation.
canon: Speaks Zhennian natively, trade-tongue carefully, and reads Avelornian only poorly.
Trained in the Crane-in-Storm spear form; anchors his breath at the sternum.
Exiled from Zhennian for breaking the unbreakable Seventh Form and beating the master he was not supposed to beat.
Carries a Shard of Prior Covenant lodged behind his sternum, given to him 17 years ago by his teacher.
Arrived in Veranthos through the Auroch Gate as the sky folded, carrying no weapon and nothing of value.
Carries his teacher's hidden third text and a carved wooden name-token recovered from Passa.
Discovered Liang Sorveth's margin-note: the fifth convergence point requires not an anchor but an answer — a living will completing the geometric circuit. An unanswered circuit is called an unasking.
Crossed the Greyveil Fault-threshold by answering the bridge's question from the body, without language — answered 'a third thing, still becoming, not yet named'
The bridge's question confirmed the utharvel reading: Ming is no longer the man who arrived unknowing, nor the Shard alone, but a third thing
Buried Liang Sorveth's third text and carved name-token in the Fault-vein at the base of the voreth-column — released the frame in clarity, not grief
Has articulated the sorveth-anak role as harmony not key: speak the Wellspring's true frequency into the re-binding geometry at the moment the Sealers close it
No longer carries the third text or the name-token
